name: Devin API (Cognition)
pricing_notes: Enterprise pricing; ~$500/mo for individual; team plans custom

performance.notes: Long-running sessions; tasks take minutes to hours; async session-based API

agent_readiness.notes: Autonomous software engineering agent. API: create session, send message, stream events, attach GitHub repos. Devin can read/write code, run tests, browse web, use terminal. Session-based async model. Webhooks for task completion.

gotchas: Very expensive — $500+/mo puts it out of reach for most users
Long task duration (minutes to hours) not suitable for real-time workflows
Sessions can fail or get stuck without clear error recovery
Code quality varies; requires human review of output
Limited customization of agent behavior vs building your own
best_when: Long-running software engineering tasks that would take hours of human effort
Automating repetitive coding tasks (migrations, refactors, test writing)
As an orchestrator in multi-agent system for code generation subtasks
Teams with budget for AI-assisted engineering at enterprise scale
avoid_when: Real-time code generation (use completion APIs like Copilot)
Tasks requiring precise control over agent behavior
Production systems where code quality must be immediately reliable
summary: Devin is Cognition's autonomous software engineering agent, marketed as the 'first AI software engineer'. API-accessible for session-based coding tasks: create a session, attach GitHub repos, send tasks, receive streamed events. Impressive at long-horizon coding tasks but expensive ($500+/mo), produces variable quality output requiring review, and isn't suitable for real-time workflows.
